title: Did Disney’s ‘Mulan’ Experiment Work? A Binge Mode Investigation
format: Sean and Amanda hand the $29.99 Disney+ Mulan remake to Binge Mode's Jason Concepcion and Mallory Rubin, who grew up on the 1998 cartoon, to work out what the live-action version loses: the songs, Mushu, and the message that an ordinary girl is enough. Plus Tenet's soft reopening weekend, a Chadwick Boseman tribute, and a look ahead to the Cruella and Little Mermaid remakes.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Opened to about $20M domestic over Labor Day weekend with New York and Los Angeles theaters still closed and rooms at 25–50% capacity. Sean and Amanda drove to a San Diego drive-in to see it; the full breakdown is held for next week.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Sean rewatched it the night after Boseman's death: a movie he carries on his shoulders, one that met impossible anticipation and lived up. Amanda cites a 2018 video of kids at Atlanta's Ron Clark Academy dancing to see it as what the film meant to young Black audiences.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: This year's Boseman role, in the Spike Lee film, offered as evidence of his eye for parts and the weight of representation.

films.title: Marshall
films.note: Boseman as Thurgood Marshall, one of a run of real-figure roles the hosts credit to his sense of complexity over easy dignity.
Boseman as Jackie Robinson, another of the biographical parts they single out.

films.title: Get on Up
films.note: Boseman as James Brown; Sean calls taking on a figure of pure talent and real complexity a bold act.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: The $29.99 Disney+ premiere. All four find it handsome but empty: no songs, Mushu swapped for a silent Phoenix, and Mulan handed magic chi powers Mallory argues gut the original's point that an ordinary girl is enough. Donnie Yen and Jet Li are wasted, Gong Li's witch dies so only one strong woman survives, and the only moment that lands is Mulan casting off her armor. Shot in Xinjiang and led by Yifei Liu, whose Hong Kong-police tweet drew a #BanMulan campaign, it became a lightning rod.

films.title: Mulan (1998)
films.note: The animated original three of them grew up on — Mallory's third-favorite Disney cartoon, I'll Make a Man Out of You her favorite Disney song. Its music, humor (Eddie Murphy's Mushu, the cricket, the grandmother) and 'you are already enough' message are exactly what the hosts say the remake drops. Sean watched it for the first time on Disney+ and enjoyed it.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: Amanda's benchmark and the reason she calls herself the world's number one Elton John fan; the songs here are the standard the Mulan music can't reach.

films.title: The Lion King (2019)
films.note: The live-action remake the hosts couldn't find a reason for beyond money: a near-duplicate that kept the songs and jokes without iterating on anything.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: The animated one; A Whole New World comes up as an all-timer, and the genie's baked-in innuendo fuels a running bit.

films.title: Aladdin (2019)
films.note: The other inert remake; its on-the-nose horniness is the trap the hosts hope the Little Mermaid remake avoids.

films.title: Beauty and the Beast
films.note: Named in the golden-age run; Something There ranks, by Amanda's count, about fifth-best among its own songs.

films.title: The Little Mermaid
films.note: Part of Your World is the song Amanda holds up as doing exposition and broad yearning at once; both hosts put it top-five Disney.

films.title: Robin Hood
films.note: Jason's actual favorite Disney songs, all written by Roger Miller — Oo-De-Lally first.

films.title: Cruella
films.note: Emma Stone as a young Cruella de Vil who becomes obsessed with dog skins; six screenwriters (including Jez Butterworth and Tony McNamara), directed by Craig Gillespie. The hosts are baffled by who it's for but sold on the cast, and Sean floats a Rocky Horror camp read.

films.title: The Little Mermaid (2023)
films.note: Rob Marshall's remake with Halle Bailey as Ariel, Melissa McCarthy as Ursula, Javier Bardem as Triton, and Daveed Diggs as Sebastian; the Diggs casting makes them hope the songs survive.

films.title: Peter Pan & Wendy
films.note: David Lowery's remake, flagged as further out; he had already done the live-action Pete's Dragon.

films: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
